article_meta.title: Supreme Court ruling against Trump’s tariffs is unlikely to mean an end to trade policy chaos 1 of 3 | Containers are st

reasoning: The model makes some correct identifications of author-level bias but commits two clear attribution rule violations (flagging quoted text from Majerus) and has some debatable type labels. It catches several genuine instances in the author's prose but also includes source quotes.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Two items flag quoted source language: item 3 ('It's only gotten more complicated for everybody' - Majerus quote) and item 8 ('The whole thing's going to be a mess' - also Majerus quote). Both are clearly inside quotation marks and attributed to a named source. This is a clear attribution rule violation in both cases.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Items 1 (stunning rebuke, sweeping, conjure up on a whim), 2 (paralyzed businesses), 4 (chaotic/unpredictability), 5 (strong-armed), 6 (handy cudgel/smack), 7 (Confusion surrounds) are all defensible as genuine author-prose bias. Items 3 and 8 are false positives due to being quoted text. So 6 of 8 are genuine, 2 are not.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model caught most of the significant author-level bias instances: 'stunning rebuke', 'conjure up on a whim', 'paralyzed businesses', 'chaotic', 'strong-armed', 'handy cudgel/smack', 'lopsided trade agreements'. It may have missed 'lopsided' as a separate instance and 'sharp-elbowed tactics' in author prose, but overall coverage is decent.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Most labels are defensible. 'Stunning rebuke' as Subjective Qualifying Adjectives is correct. 'Paralyzed businesses' as Opinion Presented as Fact is reasonable. 'Chaotic/unpredictability' as Spin works. 'Strong-armed' could be Word Choice rather than Subjective Qualifying Adjectives but is close. 'Handy cudgel/smack' as Word Choice is correct. 'Confusion surrounds' as Spin is reasonable. Some labels could be more precise but none are clearly wrong for the valid items.
